domains.domain_context_preview: ## Data collection and analysis The focus groups and telephone interviews were audiotaped and fully transcribed. Data analysis was informed by qualitative methodology (Pope and Mays, 2000). Individual questions or information needs were identified from the transcripts using the participants' own language where possible. These were discussed by both facilitators to ensure consistency of interpretation and were organised into categories to facilitate comprehension, as suggested by Ley (1988). Further focus groups and/or telephone interviews were conducted until no additional topics were raised. A draft QPL was developed using the categories and questions identified in the transcripts. The draft QPL was then reviewed by relevant health professionals and their suggestions were incorporated. No other sources of bias identified.
